name: BlockCommands
author: Swift-Strider
description: Allows commands to run when people punch, break, step, or interact with blocks.
readme_markdown: # BlockCommands [![Poggit State](https://poggit.pmmp.io/shield.state/BlockCommands)](https://poggit.pmmp.io/p/BlockCommands) [![Poggit Shield](https://poggit.pmmp.io/shield.api/BlockCommands)](https://poggit.pmmp.io/p/BlockCommands) Allows commands to run when people punch, break, step, or interact with blocks. A BlockCommand is an object that stores commands, blocks, areas, and events. When events happen in-game a BlockCommand is able to respond by running it's commands. Tips: 1. `/sudo` only runs commands if the message has a "/." 2. You can inject information of the event inside of commands - `{player}` will be replaced by the player's name when a BlockCommand is triggered. - `{position}` will be the position of the block affected. ## Usage 1. Make a new BlockCommand with `/bc create [...events]`. The `[...events]` is a list of available block events, separated by space, that will be enabled. Ex: `/bc create launchpad step interact`. - List all BlockCommands with `/bc list`. - You can delete a BlockCommand with `/bc remove `. 2. Add a command to the BlockCommand with `/bc edit addcommand `. - List commands on a BlockCommand with `/bc edit listcommands `. - Use the index of a command to remove it from a BlockCommand `/bc edit removecommand `. - Remove all commands from a BlockCommand with `/bc edit clearcommands `. 3. Add a block to the BlockCommand with `/bc edit addblock ` or `/bc edit addblock ` to add the block you're looking at. You can also add whole areas with `/bc edit addarea `. Blocks and areas that are on BlockCommands are called _attachments_. - List attachments on a BlockCommand with `/bc edit listattachments `. - Use the index from the command to remove an attachment from a BlockCommand with `/bc edit removeattachment `. - Clear all attachments from a BlockCommand with `/bc edit clearattachments `. 4. Configure the BlockCommand to run commands on different events with `/bc edit configure [...events]`. ## Features - The plugin comes with several extra commands like `/launch` to make it easy to build specific contraptions like launchpads. - Editing commands for multiple blocks is quick since they are all stored on a single BlockCommand. Commands can be run on the block events: - punch (When a player hits a block) - break (When a player destroys a block) - step (When a player first lands on a block) - interact (When a player right-clicks a block) Commands can be attached to: - Blocks - Areas ## All Commands Miscellaneous Commands - `/launch [player]` Launches the player in the given direction. Use the `[player]` argument instead of `/sudo ...` to bypass permission requirements. "/launch 2 5 0" will launch the player who ran the command in the air - `/sudo ` Sends a message as the player. If the message starts with a "/" it will be ran as a command. Great for commands that needs commands to be sent as player. This will not give the player extra permissions. `/sudo player1 /tp 0 0 0` will teleport the player to 0, 0, 0 if they have permission to use `/tp` Admin Commands _Key: `` will be the name of a BlockCommand._ - `/bc ...` You can also use /blockcommands. - `/bc help` or `/bc edit help` Fetches help for the following admin commands. - `/bc loadconfig` This reloads data from data.yml. - `/bc saveconfig` This saves data to data.yml. - `/bc create [...events]` This makes a new BlockCommand with the given block events turned on. Valid `[...events]` are: punch, break, step, interact. - `/bc list` Lists all BlockCommands. - `/bc remove ` Removes a BlockCommand. - `/bc edit configure ` Lists all the block events for the BlockCommand. - `/bc edit configure [...events]` Changes the BlockCommand events to `[...events]`. Ex: `/bc configure teleport_sign punch interact` - `/bc edit addcommand ` Adds a command to a BlockCommand. - `/bc edit listcommands ` Lists commands on a BlockCommand and their index. - `/bc edit removecommand ` Removes the command at the given index from the BlockCommand. - `/bc edit addblock ` Adds the block at ` ` to the BlockCommand. - `/bc edit addblock ` Adds the block the player is looking at to the BlockCommand. - `/bc edit addarea ` Adds an area to the block command starting from ` ` ending at ` `. - `/bc edit listattachments ` Lists the blocks and areas along with their indexes that are attached to the BlockCommand. - `/bc edit removeattachment ` Removes the block or area at `` from the BlockCommand. - `/bc edit clearattachments ` Removes all blocks and areas associated with the BlockCommand.
